source.title: Functional architecture of basal ganglia circuits: neural substrates of parallel processing.

confidence_rationale: The founding review establishing that BG are organised as several structurally and functionally distinct circuits, each linking a different region of frontal cortex through BG to thalamus and back. The authors move explicitly away from earlier serial-convergence models toward parallel functional architecture. While the paper uses the motor circuit as the principal example, it explicitly generalises: the same parallel principle is held to apply within each circuit. This is the primary source for three-gate distinctness. Confidence capped at 0.85 because the full evidence for non-motor circuits remained incomplete at this date and relied on comparisons across animals rather than within-subject tracing.

mapping.source_claim_statement: BG are organised into several structurally and functionally distinct circuits that link cortex, basal ganglia, and thalamus, with each circuit focused on a different portion of frontal lobe (motor, oculomotor, prefrontal/associative, limbic). Parallel architecture is characteristic both across circuits and within each individual circuit.
mapping.ree_translation: Directly supports the three-gate model. The motor circuit corresponds to the REE action commitment loop (putamen/VLo/SMA); the prefrontal/associative circuit to the thought loop (caudate/MD thalamus/DLPFC); the limbic circuit to the sensorium/harm-goal loop (ventral striatum/MD/OFC-ACC). The parallel architecture means these loops are anatomically segregated at every relay station, not converging to a single gate.
mapping.mapping_caveat: 1990 paper; within-subject anatomical continuity had only been demonstrated for the motor circuit. The segregated circuits hypothesis for non-motor loops was inferential at this date. Later tract-tracing and human neuroimaging substantially confirmed the architecture, but convergence zones were identified subsequently (Haber 2008, 2016). The paper explicitly acknowledges incompleteness and calls for double-label tracing.
mapping.source_context: Trends in Neurosciences, 1990. Highly cited review (>2000 citations). Sets the foundational parallel-circuits framework that remains the dominant anatomical model.

confidence_components.notes: Highest-impact review in this area. Mapping fidelity is high: the three-loop partition maps directly onto REE gates. Transfer risk is low: this is foundational primate/human neuroanatomy, not a species-specific result.
